reasoning: The question asks if Zohran Mamdani will win second place in the NYC mayoral election on November 4, 2025. This date corresponds to the general election. In NYC general mayoral elections, the winner is typically the Democratic nominee and the second-place finisher is overwhelmingly the Republican nominee. For Mamdani (a DSA-aligned Democratic State Assemblymember) to finish exactly second on November 4, he would either need to win the Democratic primary and then lose the general election to a Republican (or independent), or run on a third-party line and receive more votes than the Republican nominee but fewer than the Democrat. Both scenarios are highly improbable. Furthermore, he faces a crowded field of much more prominent citywide politicians (e.g., Brad Lander, Scott Stringer) even just to become a prominent contender. Therefore, the odds of him specifically finishing in second place in the general election are extremely low, firmly resting at the minimum probability allowed.
